questionHtml: Distinguish between the internal energy of the oxygen at the boiling point when it is in its liquid phase and when it is in its gas phase.
markschemeHtml:
Internal energy is the sum of all the PEs and KEs of the molecules (of the oxygen) ✔

PE of molecules in gaseous state is zero ✔

(At boiling point) average KE of molecules in gas and liquid is the same ✔

gases have a higher internal energy ✔

 

Molecules/particles/atoms must be included once, if not, award [1 max]

meta.title: Distinguish between the internal energy of the oxygen at the boiling point when it is in its liquid phase and when it is in its gas phase.
